description: Expedia Group is a Seattle-based online travel marketplace operating consumer travel brands and partner platforms for lodging, vacation rentals, flights, cars, cruises, activities, advertising, and travel distribution.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Expedia Group sits between travelers and travel suppliers through brands such as Expedia, Hotels.com, Vrbo, Orbitz, Travelocity, Hotwire, ebookers, CheapTickets, Wotif, CarRentals.com, Expedia Cruises, and trivago. The core offer is convenience at scale: search, compare, book, manage, and occasionally decode the fine print with more confidence than the average traveler packed into their carry-on.
The company is most visible through Expedia and Vrbo, but the registry should treat it as a broader travel-commerce stack rather than a single booking site.

overview.title: Marketplace Model
paragraphs: Expedia Group aggregates demand from travelers and routes it toward hotels, vacation-rental hosts, airlines, car-rental firms, cruise providers, activity operators, destinations, and advertisers. Its partner business extends the model through inventory distribution, travel APIs, white-label booking tools, agency programs, loyalty partnerships, and media products.

That model makes the company a gatekeeper for discovery, ranking, reviews, loyalty, payments, cancellation policies, and supplier access. The product is not just the trip; it is the mediated marketplace wrapped around the trip.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: Free The World coverage should compare Expedia Group products with direct supplier booking, open trip-planning tools, independent lodging directories, public transit and mapping resources, and community-owned destination guides where they meaningfully reduce platform dependence.

The main lock-in surfaces are account history, One Key rewards, saved traveler data, proprietary reviews, ranking opacity, bundled pricing, and exclusive distribution relationships. The convenience is real; the portability is, predictably, less well traveled.
